content.problemStatement:
Chef has AAA painters and NNN wooden boards placed in a row.

The ithi^\text{th}ith board has a length of C[i].


Each painter takes **B units of time** to paint **1 unit length** of a board.
A painter can only paint consecutive boards (no skipping allowed).
A board cannot be divided between two painters.
Chef’s goal is to **distribute the boards among painters** so that the **slowest painter finishes as early as possible**.

You need to determine the **minimum time** in which all boards can be painted. Since the answer can be very large, output it modulo `10000003`.

Function DeclarationFunction NameminTimeminTimeminTime – This function calculates the minimum time required to paint all boards when painters work under given constraints.

Parameters
AAA : The number of painters available.
BBB : The time taken by a painter to paint one unit length of a board.
CCC : A vector of integers where `C[i]` represents the length of the `i`-th board.
Return Value
Returns a number representing the **minimum time** required to paint all boards.
Since the result can be very large, the returned value should be taken **modulo `10000003`**.
Constraints
1≤A≤10001 \le A \le 10001≤A≤1000
1≤B≤1061 \le B \le 10^61≤B≤106
1≤N≤1051 \le N \le 10^51≤N≤105 where N=∣C∣N = |C|N=∣C∣
1≤C[i]≤1061 \le C[i] \le 10^61≤C[i]≤106
Each painter can paint **only consecutive boards**
A board **cannot be divided** between painters

Input Format


The first line contains three integers:



AAA → number of painters,
BBB → time taken to paint one unit length,
NNN → number of boards.



The second line contains NNN integers, where the `i-th` integer denotes the length of the `i-th` board.





Output Format
Print a single integer — the minimum time required to finish painting all the boards, modulo 100000031000000310000003.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Painter 1 -> [10, 10] -> 20 units -> 60 time
Painter 2 -> [10, 10] -> 20 units -> 60 time
Maximum = 60 -> answer = 60

Explanation:
Boards can be split as:

Painter 1 -> [5]
Painter 2 -> [2, 4] -> 6
Painter 3 -> [6]
Painter 4 -> [1, 3] -> 4
Maximum = 6
